title: thymus serpyllum f. vallesiacus
intro: Thymus serpyllum f. vallesiacus, also known as Valerian Thyme or Thyme of the Alps, is a subspecies of the Thymus serpyllum plant, a low-growing, flowering perennial native to the mountains of Europe. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Thymus serpyllum f. vallesiacus include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote a restful sleep, making it a popular herbal remedy for insomnia and other sleep disorders. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
